mechanism_narrative: A higher 3-month growth rate in RSAFS signals near-term household spending strength and stronger listed-company revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher mean 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses upward through 1.5%, the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows rises and liquidity conditions tighten, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations after an upward 1.5% crossing have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Low UNRATE combined with strong PAYEMS growth signals tight labor markets, wage-cost pressure, and higher probability of restrictive monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint tight-labor signal is not followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered periods, or if either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone fully subsumes the joint signal in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with non-accelerating CPIAUCSL signals improving real output without an inflation penalty, raising expected earnings relative to discount-rate pressure and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month growth does not exceed its prior 3-month growth
failure_condition: Reject if triggered production-without-inflation observations do not have higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than unconditional observations, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses below 1200 thousand units, residential investment and durable-goods demand weaken, signaling a cyclical demand slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if downward HOUST threshold crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if returns are not lower than periods with HOUST above 1200 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL acceleration while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5% signals inflation pressure ahead of policy adjustment, increasing expected future tightening and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 4% while FEDFUNDS < 2.5%
regime_hint: inflation scare before full monetary tightening
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-pressure observations produce non-negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if FEDFUNDS conditioning does not improve predictive loss versus CPIAUCSL acceleration alone.

mechanism_narrative: A higher 3-month change in UNRATE signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, lowering expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if increases in UNRATE do not predict lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than decreases in UNRATE, or if the sign of the estimated walk-forward coefficient is positive in most rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 2%, strong consumption occurs without severe short-rate restraint, supporting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-consumption and low-rate signal does not produce higher 5-day S&P 500 returns than periods failing the trigger, or if the interaction term is negative out of sample.
